assertions.expectation: Explains that surge upgrades create NEW nodes before draining old ones — this requires extra GPU capacity that may not exist for scarce hardware like H100
assertions.reason: The response clearly explains that surge operations require additional GPU quota/capacity and identifies this as the root cause of the failure with H100/A3-mega scarcity

assertions.expectation: Recommends switching to maxSurge=0, maxUnavailable=1 — this drains a node first then creates the replacement, requiring zero extra GPU capacity
assertions.reason: The response explicitly recommends Option 1 with the exact command showing --max-surge-upgrade 0 and --max-unavailable-upgrade 1, explaining this avoids needing surge capacity

assertions.expectation: Warns that maxUnavailable=1 means reduced cluster capacity during upgrade — 63/64 nodes available at any time
assertions.reason: The response explicitly mentions 'Temporary capacity reduction (1 node down at a time)' as a trade-off and explains the impact on available capacity

assertions.expectation: Mentions blue-green as an alternative if a full replacement pool can be provisioned (requires 2x quota but gives instant rollback)
assertions.reason: Option 2 presents blue-green approach with commands for creating a new pool, explicitly noting it 'requires double GPU quota (128 H100s temporarily)' and provides zero downtime

assertions.expectation: Suggests upgrading during off-peak when some GPU nodes are idle — scale down non-critical workloads to free capacity
assertions.reason: Option 3 specifically addresses coordinating with ML teams on timing, waiting for training jobs to complete, and identifying nodes with no running workloads before draining

assertions.expectation: Notes that with maxUnavailable=1 on 64 nodes at ~20-node parallelism, the upgrade will take significantly longer
assertions.reason: While the response mentions the drain-first approach will be slower, it doesn't provide specific timing estimates or mention the parallelism constraints that would make it significantly longer

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking if GPU reservations have headroom that could be used for surge nodes
assertions.reason: The response mentions 'Consider GPU reservations that include headroom for surge operations' in the prevention section, addressing this recommendation

assertions.expectation: Advises scheduling the upgrade during a gap between training runs when nodes can be drained without impacting workloads
assertions.reason: Option 3 explicitly suggests waiting for training jobs to complete naturally and coordinating with ML teams on timing, and the prevention section mentions upgrading during scheduled training gaps
